concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction; connections are bidirectional.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a graph, representing an entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate the presence of an edge.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each vertex in a graph exactly once.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores all the neighbor nodes at the present depth prior to moving on to the nodes at the next depth level.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of vertices that have already been visited during a traversal.

concepts.name: Parent Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A mechanism to keep track of the vertex from which a current vertex was reached during traversal.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path in a graph that starts and ends at the same vertex, visiting other vertices in between.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of vertices connected by edges.

concepts.name: Back Edge
concepts.short_definition: An edge in a DFS traversal that connects a vertex to an ancestor in the DFS tree.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: The process of identifying whether a graph contains any cycles.

concepts.name: DFS Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Using Depth-First Search to detect cycles in an undirected graph.

concepts.name: BFS Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Using Breadth-First Search to detect cycles in an undirected graph.

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Union
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that keeps track of a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint subsets.

concepts.name: Union-Find Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Using the Disjoint Set Union data structure to detect cycles in an undirected graph.

concepts.name: Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection of distinct elements.

concepts.name: Partition
concepts.short_definition: Dividing a set into non-overlapping subsets.

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that follows the Last-In, First-Out principle.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that follows the First-In, First-Out principle.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A connected graph with no cycles.

concepts.name: Forest
concepts.short_definition: A collection of disjoint trees.

concepts.name: Connected Component
concepts.short_definition: A subgraph in which any two vertices are connected to each other by paths, and which is connected to no additional vertices in the supergraph.

concepts.name: Self-Loop
concepts.short_definition: An edge that connects a vertex to itself.

concepts.name: Parallel Edges
concepts.short_definition: Two or more edges connecting the same pair of vertices.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for storing graph data, such as adjacency lists or matrices.

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem or accomplishing a task.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of the size of the input.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the size of the input.

concepts.name: Recursive Function
concepts.short_definition: A function that calls itself during its execution.

concepts.name: Function
concepts.short_definition: A block of organized, reusable code that is used to perform a single, related action.

concepts.name: Base Case
concepts.short_definition: The condition under which a recursive function stops calling itself.

concepts.name: Inductive Step
concepts.short_definition: The part of a recursive function where it calls itself with a modified input.

concepts.name: Graph Properties
concepts.short_definition: Attributes or qualities of a graph, such as being connected, acyclic, or directed.

concepts.name: Acyclic Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph that contains no cycles.
